role: Commander-in-Chief, Law Enforcement Command of Iran (2022–present)
bio: Ahmad-Reza Radan is the Commander-in-Chief of the [Law Enforcement Command of Iran](/orgs/law-enforcement-command), appointed in January 2023 by [Ali Khamenei](/people/ali-khamenei). A veteran security official, Radan previously served as deputy commander of Iran's police force during the [2009 Green Movement protests](/events/green-movement-protests-2009), where he oversaw the violent crackdown on demonstrators. He is under EU and US sanctions for his role in the suppression of protests. During the [January 2026 massacres](/events/january-2026-massacres), police forces under his command used live ammunition against protesters as part of the coordinated security response ordered by the Supreme National Security Council.
editorial_notes: EU-sanctioned since 2011 for role in 2009 crackdown. US-sanctioned. Appointed Commander-in-Chief of FARAJA in January 2023.
